human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, authority-source policy, scenario coverage, results, readiness review, deployment notes, and marketing notes for consistency with issue #152 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to managed file transfer and EDI exchange guidance, legacy-platform modernization, partner onboarding, acknowledgment handling, and replay-recovery workflows.
Real public authoritative sources are documented with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy in manifest.yaml.
The package defines specialist-owned shared-meta-agent contracts, specialty-specific deltas, and conservative fallback behavior when delegated outputs are unavailable, stale, or low-confidence.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local source baseline.
Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment normalization, and commercialization normalization are delegated to #302 through #305 with local-artifact fallback.
Authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffold consistency review are delegated to #306 through #308 without weakening the local baseline.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #152.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation, monitoring and logging expectations, rollback path, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, licensing constraints around proprietary implementation guides, proof points, and go-to-market gaps.
